# meltcloud_elastic_node_pool (Data Source)

An Elastic Node Pool is a grouping of Elastic Fleet nodes scheduled into a consuming organization's cluster, sized via node count and per-node resources.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# get elastic node pool by ID
data "meltcloud_elastic_node_pool" "example" {
  cluster_id = 1
  id         = 42
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_id` (Number) ID of the cluster the node pool runs on
- `id` (Number) Internal ID of the Elastic Node Pool on meltcloud

### Read-Only

- `elastic_quota_id` (Number) ID of the Elastic Quota backing the node pool
- `name` (String) Name of the Elastic Node Pool
- `node_config` (Attributes) Per-node resource configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--node_config))
- `node_count` (Number) Number of nodes in the node pool
- `patch_version` (String) Kubernetes patch version of the Elastic Node Pool nodes (Kubelet)
- `status` (String) Status of the Elastic Node Pool
- `version` (String) Kubernetes minor version of the Elastic Node Pool nodes (Kubelet)

<a id="nestedatt--node_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `node_config`

Read-Only:

- `cores` (Number) Number of cores per node
- `disk_gb` (Number) Disk in GB per node
- `memory_mb` (Number) Memory in MB per node
